high.claim: spec claims Linear two-way sync — webhook triggers builds AND state updates flow back to Linear
high.reality: webhook.py implements only inbound Linear webhook parsing (verify_linear_signature, parse_linear_event, linear_issue_to_brief). There are zero outbound Linear API calls — no issueUpdate GraphQL mutation, no linear_update_issue function, no Linear API client instantiation anywhere in the provided files. The sync is one-directional: Linear → Pi CEO only.
high.recommendation: Implement an outbound Linear client (e.g. POST to https://api.linear.app/graphql with issueUpdate mutation) and call it from the build completion path to transition issue state back to 'done' or 'in review'.

high.claim: spec implies build logs stream to browser in real time via SSE endpoint that pipes session.output_lines
high.reality: Neither sessions.py nor main.py defines or registers an SSE endpoint. main.py includes sessions.router, webhooks.router, pipeline.router, and 14 other routers but none are named for SSE/log-streaming. The em() helper is imported and used in kill_session (event emission to in-memory store), but no HTTP endpoint that delivers those events to the browser via Server-Sent Events is visible in the provided files. Only a WebSocket path (/ws/build/{sid}) is referenced in the probe context.
high.recommendation: Add a GET /api/sessions/{sid}/stream route (SSE, text/event-stream) in routes/sessions.py that reads session.output_lines and yields them as data: frames, then include the router in main.py.

low.reality: The route.ts file is truncated before the POST handler and command-dispatch logic is reached. Helper functions for /build (triggerBuild) and /status (getPiCeoStatus) exist, but no /start or /help command handlers are visible in the provided source excerpt. Cannot confirm /start or /help are wired.
low.recommendation: Confirm the full POST handler contains explicit branches for /start and /help text commands; if absent, add minimal handlers that reply with an intro and command list respectively.

low.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
low.reality: The tests/ directory contains only unit tests (test_auth.py: 13 auth tests, test_webhook.py: ~14 webhook signature tests). No pytest-based E2E or integration tests covering the build pipeline (sessions, evaluator, push) exist in the test suite — coverage for those paths relies entirely on smoke_test.py which requires a live server and is not run in CI as a standard pytest suite.
low.recommendation: Add a pytest integration test module (e.g. tests/test_pipeline_e2e.py) that spins up the server in-process and exercises the session→evaluator→push flow end-to-end, rather than relying solely on the live-server smoke script.

low.claim: RA-1154: pipeline probe — canary marker, no functional change (listed under Sprint 10 complete)
low.reality: The code comment in smoke_test.py reads explicitly 'RA-1154: pipeline probe — canary marker, no functional change', confirming this PR added no actual test logic — it was a marker commit only. The spec counting it as a completed pipeline coverage item overstates what was delivered.
low.recommendation: Remove RA-1154 from the 'E2E coverage complete' checklist or replace the canary comment with a real pipeline probe assertion (e.g. a /api/pipeline/{id} status check in the 22-check suite).

low.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
low.reality: The --agent-sdk flag that triggers 'SDK path verification checks (RA-575)' is opt-in (action='store_true', defaults to False). Standard smoke test runs invoked without that flag skip all SDK path verification, so the claimed complete SDK coverage is not exercised by default.
low.recommendation: Either make --agent-sdk the default when TAO_USE_AGENT_SDK=1 is set in the environment, or split SDK checks into a separate always-on check block so they are never silently omitted.

low.claim: BudgetTracker in src/tao/budget/tracker.py is a correct implementation, but session_sdk.py does not instantiate it — SDK metric rows capture output_len but not tokens_used
low.reality: create_session in sessions.py imports budget_to_params and describe_budget for parameter tuning (model, thresholds) but never imports or instantiates BudgetTracker. Token-level tracking (tokens_used field) is therefore absent from SDK metric rows, consistent with the spec's own admission that the tracker is unconnected.
low.recommendation: In session_sdk.py, instantiate BudgetTracker at session start, increment it from the structured SDK response's usage field after each query call, and write the final tokens_used value into the SDK metric row.

low.claim: kill_session is a complete session lifecycle operation
low.reality: kill_session has no except clause around the terminate/kill block; any OSError or ProcessLookupError (e.g. process already exited) will propagate uncaught to the caller rather than returning False gracefully.
low.recommendation: Wrap the terminate/kill/status block in try/except (OSError, ProcessLookupError) and return False on error so callers receive a clean boolean rather than an unhandled exception.
